title: amaryllis belladonna var. major
intro: Amaryllis belladonna var. major, also known as the major trumpet amaryllis or giant amaryllis, is a large, showy flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the bulb of this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Amaryllis belladonna var. major is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction in herbal remedies, and its extracts are also used in homeopathic and traditional medicine.
